label: Typika (Obednitsa)
description: The Reader's Office of the Typika (Obednitsa). Celebrated in the absence of a priest or Divine Liturgy on Sundays and feasts, in place of Holy Communion. Consists of the Beatitudes, Psalms 102 and 145, the Symbol of Faith, the Trisagion Prayers, and the Lord's Prayer, with appointed Epistle and Gospel readings.

sections.label: Opening
sections.notes: The Typika opens with the Usual Beginning, as do all Byzantine offices.

items.text:
The Priest blesses: Blessed is our God, always, now and ever and unto ages of ages.
If no Priest is present, the Reader begins: Through the prayers of our holy fathers, Lord Jesus Christ our God, have mercy on us and save us. Amen.

items.label: The Usual Beginning
items.note: Trisagion prayers sequence. Resolved from typika-fixed.json by HorologionEngine.

sections.label: The Beatitudes
sections.notes: The Beatitudes (Matthew 5:3–12) with refrains. Appointed at the Typika in place of the Antiphons of the Liturgy.

items.label: The Beatitudes (Matthew 5:3–12)
items.note: Fixed text. Resolved from typika-fixed.json by HorologionEngine.

sections.label: Psalms 102 and 145
sections.notes: Two psalms appointed at the Typika: Psalm 102 (LXX) — Bless the Lord, O my soul; and Psalm 145 (LXX) — Praise the Lord, O my soul.

items.label: Psalm 102 (LXX) — Bless the Lord, O my soul
Psalm 145 (LXX) — Praise the Lord, O my soul

sections.label: Symbol of Faith
sections.notes: The Nicene-Constantinopolitan Creed is recited at the Typika.

sections.label: Troparion of the Day
sections.notes: The dismissal troparion of the day, resolved dynamically from the Menaion/Octoechos stack.

items.label: Troparion of the Day
items.note: Variable. Resolved via existing Menaion/Octoechos troparion stack by HorologionEngine.

items.text: KONTAKION: The appointed kontakion of the day is read here. On ordinary weekdays, the kontakion of the saint from the Menaion; on Sundays and great feasts, the kontakion of the feast. If no proper kontakion is appointed, the kontakion to the Theotokos is read: 'To thee, the Champion Leader, we thy servants dedicate a feast of victory and of thanksgiving, as ones rescued out of sufferings, O Theotokos: but as thou art one with might which is invincible, from all dangers that can be do thou deliver us, that we may cry to thee: Rejoice, thou Bride unwedded.'

sections.label: Epistle and Gospel
sections.notes: The appointed Epistle and Gospel for the day are read. Appointments follow the Apostol and Evangelist.

items.text: EPISTLE: The appointed Epistle reading for the day from the Apostol is read here. Consult the Apostol for the appointed pericope according to the day and tone.
GOSPEL: The appointed Gospel reading for the day from the Evangelist is read here. Consult the Evangelist for the appointed pericope according to the day and tone.

sections.label: Lord's Prayer and Dismissal
sections.notes: The Lord's Prayer is read. A structural dismissal rubric closes the office.

items.text:
Lord, have mercy. (×3)
Glory to the Father, and to the Son, and to the Holy Spirit, both now and ever, and unto ages of ages. Amen.

If a Priest is present: the Priest gives the dismissal according to the day and feast.
If no Priest is present: Through the prayers of our holy fathers, Lord Jesus Christ our God, have mercy on us and save us. Amen.
